acceptance_targets: [CH10_CRAFT_UNDERPLAYED_BETRAYAL] PASS if the revised exchange in snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:213-349 lets a cold reader state both of these facts from the page itself: Margot altered the set after something was visibly wrong, and Lena confronts her about that action directly. FAIL if the scene still confines the dispute to whether the incident qualifies as Class III and what filing would cost, without forcing Margot to answer for pushing Lena harder during the missing-time stretch.

[CH10_DIALOGUE_COMPOSED_CORRIDOR_SCENE] PASS if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:239-349 still conveys the reporting stakes but no longer contains policy-brief paragraphs longer than two sentences without interruption, and at least two of Margot's key evasions arrive as cutoffs, redirected answers, or refusals rather than polished maxims. FAIL if the scene still reads as a chain of thesis statements, quotable reversals, and clean rhetorical counters.

[CH10_CONCEALMENT_TEMPLATE_REPETITION] Pass if the revised chapter's first half no longer follows the same sequential template as Chapter 4 (enter feed session, athlete anomaly during monitoring, observe through interface, concealment decision). The Class III event and Margot's concealment must still occur, but should arrive through a different structural path. Fail if a reader familiar with Chapter 4 can predict the sequence of the revised chapter's opening half from the earlier template.
[R024] Ch10 contains no more than 4 instances of the word 'display.'

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised exchange in snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:213-349 lets a cold reader state both of these facts from the page itself: Margot altered the set after something was visibly wrong, and Lena confronts her about that action directly. FAIL if the scene still confines the dispute to whether the incident qualifies as Class III and what filing would cost, without forcing Margot to answer for pushing Lena harder during the missing-time stretch.
findings.problem: The chapter correctly shows Lena discovering that Margot changed the pace target after the set went wrong, and it even names the uglier interpretation: Margot may have felt something wrong and pushed harder anyway. But the actual confrontation does not force that betrayal into the open. Once Lena reaches Margot, the scene shifts almost entirely into classification and filing consequences, so the most damning bodily violation on the page becomes subtext instead of a pressed accusation. That is timidity at the chapter's point of maximum relational consequence: the scene argues about paperwork when the reader has already seen a coach use an absent athlete's body for the feed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the confrontation at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:213, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:239, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:289 so Lena explicitly brings the unauthorized pace increase from snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:181-187 into the conversation before or during the Class III dispute. Margot should have to answer the more personal charge as well as the administrative one: either admit she pushed, deny it, or substitute a partial truth in a way the page makes legible as evasion. Keep the institutional-stakes material, but only after the scene has made the coach-athlete exploitation concrete in dialogue rather than leaving it to the reader to import from the first section.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:239-349 still conveys the reporting stakes but no longer contains policy-brief paragraphs longer than two sentences without interruption, and at least two of Margot's key evasions arrive as cutoffs, redirected answers, or refusals rather than polished maxims. FAIL if the scene still reads as a chain of thesis statements, quotable reversals, and clean rhetorical counters.
findings.problem: The chapter's main dialogue scene is too composed for its pressure. The audit sub-modes all show up: thesis-speech ('the record doesn't know that'; 'the system is what decides whether you train tomorrow'), aphoristic or quotable lines ('It sounds institutional because it is institutional'; 'That is not the same sentence'), mini-speech diagnosis (Margot's filing-risk brief), and mic-drop rhetorical shaping ('And what does it buy you?'). Lena and Margot remain distinguishable, but the exchange sounds written-about rather than spoken-through. Instead of interruptions, defensive mess, or evasive half-answers, the scene keeps producing polished two-line arguments that could survive a copy edit unchanged.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the conversation across snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:239-349 so the information survives but the speech gets less balanced and less quotable. Break Margot's administrative explanations at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:279-283 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:325-337 into pressured, character-true turns with interruption, redirection, or refusal. Preserve Lena's moderate-contraction, slightly formal precision from the style bible, but strip out the neat thesis-line cadence at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:315-317 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_10.md:341-345 so the scene sounds like two people in a corridor, not two positions in an argument brief.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised chapter's first half no longer follows the same sequential template as Chapter 4 (enter feed session, athlete anomaly during monitoring, observe through interface, concealment decision). The Class III event and Margot's concealment must still occur, but should arrive through a different structural path. Fail if a reader familiar with Chapter 4 can predict the sequence of the revised chapter's opening half from the earlier template.

evidence.excerpt: 2950: Her voice came up through the audio pickup, flat and tinny in the tile room. No one answered. Margot stayed in the chair and watched Lena look at the water clock, then at the lane line, then back at the wall as if some missing piece might still be hanging there in the air above it.

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
2882: # Chapter 10
2883: 
2884: The pool was empty except for Lena.
2885: 
2886: Margot settled into the coaching chair on the observation deck and woke the session display. It was six forty-five a.m. The facility hadn't come online yet. The sprinters would not start until eight. The calibration bays were dark. Below the glass, the pool lay flat under the overheads, ten lanes of hard blue light. Lena stood on the far deck pulling on her cap. When she tucked her hair back, the port at the base of her skull showed for a second: a small round disc, skin-colored, then hidden again.
2887: 
[...]
3045: 
3046: The telemetry said her body had done the rest. Her arms had pulled. Her legs had kicked. The walls had registered contact. Her heart rate had gone into the 190s. The split graph showed a sudden hard middle build she had never been assigned. All of it was real. None of it had been experienced.
3047: 
3048: Smaller slips had happened before. Twenty minutes gone in her room while a page sat open in front of her. A turn pattern last season's firmware chose for her. Margot had called those within acceptable parameters, and Lena had accepted it because Margot had the data and the authorit...

findings.problem: Chapter 10's dramatic architecture recycles Chapter 4's template: Margot enters a live feed session with an athlete, the monitored athlete experiences a system anomaly during the session, Margot observes the crisis through the neural interface at sensory resolution, and the chapter's primary tension is whether she will conceal or report the event. Chapter 4 runs this sequence with Tomas (hurdle anomaly at HR 161, Margot conceals). Chapter 10 runs the same sequence with Lena (desync at HR 195, Class III event, Margot conceals). The escalation from first to second concealment is real—the stakes are higher, the athlete is closer to Margot personally, and the event classification is more severe. Chapter 10's second half introduces genuinely new material absent from Chapter 4: Lena investigates, discovers Margot's pattern, and confronts her directly, producing a scene whose dramatic shape has no precedent in the earlier chapter. But the opening half follows the feed-session-anomaly-concealment sequence closely enough that a reader who tracked the pattern in Chapter 4 will recognize the template before it resolves, reducing the chapter's early tension to the question of how bad this concealment will be rather than whether it will happen. The predictability of the template undercuts what should be a pivotal escalation in Margot's arc.
findings.rewrite_direction: Restructure snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2882-3050 so the chapter's first half is structurally distinct from Chapter 4's feed-session-anomaly-concealment sequence. Options: (a) open mid-crisis or in immediate aftermath—the Class III event already occurred, and the chapter begins with Margot processing what she observed and deciding what to do, skipping the approach-and-monitoring template entirely; (b) shift focalization so that Lena's growing awareness of something wrong drives the opening rather than Margot's feed perspective, arriving at the concealment through the person being concealed from rather than the person concealing; (c) intercut the feed session with material from another timeline or POV strand so that the feed-anomaly-concealment beats do not play out in the same unbroken sequence as Chapter 4. The chapter should still contain Margot's concealment and the Class III event, but the dramatic path to those beats should be visibly different from Chapter 4's.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch10 contains no more than 4 instances of the word 'display.'
findings.evidence: 'display' appears 7 times in ch10 (lines 2882-3324). Manuscript total: 55.
findings.problem: Ch10 uses 'display' 7 times. Margot's coaching-feed scenes and the session-display interface motivate screen references, but 7 in one chapter compounds the manuscript-wide overuse.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace 3-4 of the 7 'display' instances with 'screen,' 'console,' or implied interface references. Keep 'display' for the wall-mounted diagnostic or session display specifically.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
